titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/57

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=523 status=live modified=2021-04-07 07:59:04.59717+00 | translation moa=522 status=live modified=2021-04-07 07:58:49.192881+00

div.p:
W. dei gracia Rex Scott’ . Omnibus probis hominibus Tocius terre sue clericis et laicis Salutem . Sciant presentes et futuri me concessisse et Hac Carta mea
confirmasse deo et ecclesie Sancte Marie . de Melros . et monachis ibidem deo seruientibus donacionem illam quam auus et pater . Comitis patricii .
et Comes Patricius eis fecerint . de hertesheued . et de Spot . per rectas diuisas predictarum terrarum . inter predictos monachos et steintun . quas habue
runt temporibus aui et patris predicti comitis . Patricii . et que nominantur in confirmacionem prefati Comitis . patricii . in Boscho . et plano . in pratis . et pascuis . et omnibus aliis ad predictas terras iuste pertinentibus . et preterea cum communi pastura de Mora . ad tres greges arietum . ita ut unusquisque
Grex contineat . Quingentos arietes . cum aisiamentis eiusdem more . Preterea . concedo et hac Carta mea confirmo . prefatis monachis
illas Quinque acras terre . quas prefatus Comes . Patricius . eis dedit in incrementum in australi parte de Mosiburnerig . ut includant eas fossato
ad faciendum Berkariam ad prefatos tres greges . arietum . et illis Quinque acris terre utentur sicut melius eis uisum fuerit . ita tamen ut
eas non colant . Et preterea . quicquid iuris prefatus comes . patricius . uel heredes sui uel homines sui habebant uel habere poterant de communi
in prato illo quod prefati Monachi tenent . de Militibus Alani . filio Walteri in feudo de Inuerwic’ . subtus Brunrig . que prefatus . co
mes Patricius . prefatis monachis concessit . et carta sua confirmauit . Tenend’ . in liberam et puram et perpetuam elemosinam ita li
bere . et Quiete . plenarie et honorifice . sicut Carta prefati Comitis . Patricii . Testatur . Saluo seruicio meo . Test’ . Hug’.
Cancellario . meo . Willelmo . de Moruill’ . constabulario . philippo . de valon’ . Camerario . Willelmo . Cumin . Iohanne . de hasting’ . Ricardo . Marescallo .
Roberto de Burneuill’ . dauid de Haia . Rob’ . de Moruill’ . Apud . Berewic’ . xvi . die April .

William by the grace of God king of Scots to all worthy men of the whole of his land clerk and lay greeting. Let present and future know me to have granted and by this my charter to have made firm to God and to the church of St Mary at Melrose and to the monks in that place serving God the donation which the grandfather and father of Earl Patrick as well as Earl Patrick made to them at Hartside and at Spott by the aforesaid correct bounds of the lands between the aforesaid monks and Stenton which they had in the time of the grandfather and father of the aforesaid Earl Patrick and which are named in the confirmation of the aforementioned Earl Patrick, in wood and plain, in meadows and pastures, and in all other things justly pertaining to the aforesaid lands; and in addition with common pasture of the moor for three flocks of rams, so that each flock should contain five hundred rams, with easements of the same moor; in addition I grant and by this my charter make firm to the aforementioned monks the five acres of land which the aforementioned Earl Patrick gave to them as an enlargement on the south side of 'Mosiburnerig' that they enclose them by ditch and bank to make a sheepfold for the aforementioned three flocks of rams; and the five acres of land may be used just as it seems best to them, yet so that they should not till them; and in addition whatever right of common the aforementioned Earl Patrick, or his heirs or his vassals, used to have or could have in the meadowland which the aforementioned monks hold of the knights of Alan fitz Walter in the feu of Innerwick, below Brunrig, which the aforementioned Earl Patrick granted to the aforementioned monks and made firm by his charter; to be held in free and pure and everlasting alms as freely and quitely, fully and honourably, as the charter of the aforementioned Earl Patrick bears witness; saving my service. As witnesses: Hugh my Chancellor; William de Morville, Constable; Philip de Valognes, Chamberlain; William Comyn; John of Hastings; Richard Marshall; Robert de Bourneville; David de la Haye; Robert de Morville. At Berwick on the sixteenth day of April.
